description: Sprint 20 Task 37.4 — director-LUT-anchors mapped to CSS filter values. Each preset reflects the director's cinema_palette + motion_signature from designers/<id>.md. Values are tuned to be visually distinguishable from each other 11 (DINOv2-cosine spread ≥ 0.4 target).

wong-kar-wai.rationale: Warm amber bias (positive hue-rotate toward orange) + slight saturation lift for the neon-symbolic against cool shadow. Subtle sepia for memory-smear feel. Mild brightness pull-down for nighttime intimacy.

villeneuve.rationale: Cool desaturation (negative hue-rotate toward cyan, sat < 1) + high contrast for monolithic dread. No sepia — Villeneuve avoids warmth. Brightness pulled down 10% for the sand-storm haze and concrete-bunker mood.

wes-anderson.rationale: Lifted pastels via saturation boost + gentle sepia warmth for the hand-painted storybook palette. Reduced contrast keeps the symmetric flatness; brightness lift opens up the highlights without blowing them out.

nolan.rationale: Steel-blue desaturation (slight negative hue + low sat) for the IMAX-grade procedural mood. Higher contrast than Villeneuve but less crushed. No sepia — Nolan reads as cold-clinical, not nostalgic.

kubrick.rationale: Neutral hue (Kubrick uses primary color-blocking, not a global tint) with elevated contrast for the formal-rigour symmetry. Slight saturation lift makes red-room reds pop. Zero sepia, zero brightness shift — Kubrick is geometrically neutral.

lynch.rationale: Strong sodium-vapour orange shift (largest positive hue-rotate in the catalogue) + heavy contrast and sepia for the dread-inducing dream-logic. Brightness drops 15% to bury detail in shadow. The most aggressive grade.

tarkovsky.rationale: Heaviest sepia in the catalogue + low saturation for the iconic sepia-into-color long-take palette. Warm hue-shift for autumn-light + water-and-rust tones. Brightness slightly down for the contemplative damp-earth mood.

denis.rationale: Tropical-noir warmth with gentle hue-shift toward red + saturation lift for sweat-and-skin tactile palettes. Mild sepia adds the humid-celluloid feel. Brightness slightly up to keep the high-key tropical light in highlights.

bong.rationale: Slight cool-shift + mild contrast lift for the rain-soaked vertical-stratification palette. Near-neutral saturation (Bong tonal-whiplash works because the base palette stays naturalistic). Brightness pulled down 6% for the basement-dread layers.

parker.rationale: Highest saturation in the catalogue + brightness lift for the high-key musical-theatre palette. Slight warm hue-shift + light sepia for the period-musical staging. The 'spotlight on stage' grade.

garland.rationale: Highest contrast in the catalogue + cool desaturation + raised brightness for the clinical white + saturated alarm machine-precision palette (Ex Machina, Annihilation, Devs). No sepia — Garland is the antithesis of nostalgic warmth.

coppola.rationale: Soft-focus melancholia via lowest contrast in the catalogue + warm sepia + mild desaturation. Slight warm hue-shift + brightness lift produces the hazy pastel intimacy of Lost in Translation / Marie Antoinette / The Virgin Suicides.
